ops.title: ast-grep as PATH dependency, not vendored
ops.body:
Chose PATH dependency over vendoring for ast-grep (v0.22.0, first phase of the tilth rip-and-replace): no per-platform checksums to bump, aligned with how ripgrep is treated, and the Homebrew formula already carries `depends_on "ast-grep"`.

- Resolution is `lookpath.Find("ast-grep")` only — never `sg` (shadow-utils setgroups on Linux).
- A runtime version floor (0.44.0) is enforced in `internal/astgrep/resolve.go` via one `--version` probe, memoized per binary path; failures re-probe so a binary fixed in place recovers without restarting the resident MCP proxy.
- CI installs `ast-grep-cli` via uv, unpinned — the anchor canary is the drift alarm for upstream grammar changes.
- The trade: automatic availability (auto-download on first use) is gone; a missing binary is a hard error with an install hint. Re-vendoring would reintroduce checksum-bump toil across 5 platforms.
ops.when: Before re-vendoring a tool or adding a new binary dependency to the project
